impacts.title: Registration and authentication
impacts.description: The page explicitly states 'Xperience does not support roles and role management as part of the Identity integration' and lists NoOpApplicationRole/NoOpApplicationRoleStore as empty implementations. This is now factually wrong — the commit introduces member roles via ApplicationRole and ApplicationRoleStore. The code sample uses NoOpApplicationRole in AddIdentity<> which must be updated to ApplicationRole. The architecture section must describe the new role-based access model.
impacts.scope: 1) Remove/rewrite the bullet point about NoOpApplicationRole/NoOpApplicationRoleStore (line 35). Replace with explanation of ApplicationRole and ApplicationRoleStore for member role management. 2) Update the AddIdentity<ApplicationUser, NoOpApplicationRole> code snippet (line 116) to use ApplicationRole. 3) Add a new section or subsection explaining the three-tier content access model (Public / Secured / SecuredWithRoles). 4) Update the 'Xperience ASP.NET Identity architecture' section to mention role stores. 5) Update 'Configure registration and authentication' to include role-related service registration. 6) Add cross-reference to new member roles page(s).

impacts.title: Forms authentication
impacts.description: Contains three code samples using NoOpApplicationRole and NoOpApplicationRoleStore (lines 114, 133, 138, 191). All must be updated to ApplicationRole/ApplicationRoleStore. The Prerequisites section may need to mention role setup.
impacts.scope: 1) Replace all AddIdentity<ApplicationUser, NoOpApplicationRole> with AddIdentity<ApplicationUser, ApplicationRole> in code samples. 2) Replace AddRoleStore<NoOpApplicationRoleStore> with AddRoleStore<ApplicationRoleStore>. 3) Add note about role store registration in prerequisites or configuration section.

impacts.title: External authentication
impacts.description: Contains code samples using NoOpApplicationRole and NoOpApplicationRoleStore (lines 48, 53). Must be updated to ApplicationRole/ApplicationRoleStore.
impacts.scope: 1) Replace AddIdentity<ApplicationUser, NoOpApplicationRole> with ApplicationRole in code sample. 2) Replace AddRoleStore<NoOpApplicationRoleStore> with ApplicationRoleStore.

impacts.title: Add fields to member objects
impacts.description: Contains code samples using NoOpApplicationRole and NoOpApplicationRoleStore (lines 63, 68). Must be updated to ApplicationRole/ApplicationRoleStore. The 'Remarks – ApplicationUser and MemberInfo' section may need to mention that ApplicationRole maps to MemberRoleInfo.
impacts.scope: 1) Replace AddIdentity<ApplicationUser, NoOpApplicationRole> with ApplicationRole in code sample. 2) Replace AddRoleStore<NoOpApplicationRoleStore> with ApplicationRoleStore. 3) Consider adding a remark about the ApplicationRole-to-MemberRoleInfo data flow, mirroring the existing ApplicationUser-to-MemberInfo explanation.

impacts.title: Retrieve content items
impacts.description: The 'Content item security' section describes content as binary secured/unsecured and only mentions ContentItemIsSecured boolean + User.Identity.IsAuthenticated for access checks. The new member roles system adds ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames on query results and the HasAccess() extension method, which are more granular alternatives. The existing code sample checking IsAuthenticated is incomplete for role-based scenarios.
impacts.scope: 1) Update the 'Content item security' section to describe the three-tier model (public, authenticated-only, role-restricted). 2) Document ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames property available on query result containers. 3) Add code sample showing HasAccess(User) extension method as the recommended access check. 4) Update the existing IsAuthenticated check example to note it only covers binary secured/unsecured — point to HasAccess() for role-aware checks. 5) Add projection example for ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames alongside existing ContentItemIsSecured projection.

impacts.title: Retrieve page content
impacts.description: The 'Page security configuration' section describes binary secured/unsecured model with ContentItemIsSecured and User.Identity.IsAuthenticated. Needs updating for the three-tier model with role-based access. The code example showing 403 redirect via AccessDeniedPath is correct but incomplete — doesn't cover the 401 vs 403 distinction for role-based access.
impacts.scope: 1) Update 'Page security configuration' section to describe three-tier access model. 2) Document HasAccess(User) extension method as the recommended programmatic check. 3) Update code sample to show role-aware access checking. 4) Explain the distinction between NotAuthenticated (401) and Forbidden (403) responses for role-restricted content. 5) Update AccessDeniedPath example to note it handles both unauthenticated and insufficient-role scenarios.

impacts.title: Secure pages
impacts.description: Currently describes only a binary 'Requires authentication' toggle on the Security tab. The member roles feature adds role-based access restrictions — specific member roles can be required for page access. The Security tab UI likely now includes role selection in addition to the authentication toggle.
impacts.scope: 1) Update step 4 ('Configure the page's security settings') to describe the new role-based options in addition to 'Requires authentication'. 2) Document how to restrict a page to specific member roles via the Security tab. 3) Update screenshots showing the new Security tab UI with role selection. 4) Add explanation of the three-tier model in business-user terms: public, signed-in only, specific member roles. 5) Add cross-reference to the Members application for role management.

impacts.title: Content items
impacts.description: The 'Secure content items' section (line 349) describes only 'Requires authentication' as the security option. With member roles, content items can now be restricted to specific roles, not just any authenticated user. The Security tab screenshots need updating.
impacts.scope: 1) Update 'Secure content items' section to describe role-based security options alongside 'Requires authentication'. 2) Document how to restrict content items to specific member roles via Security settings. 3) Update the description 'Secured content items require that the consumer is signed in' to also cover role-restricted scenarios. 4) Update screenshots showing the expanded Security settings UI. 5) Add cross-reference to the new member roles management page.

impacts.title: Members
impacts.description: The Members page describes only basic member management (view details, disable/delete). With member roles, the Members application now includes role management capabilities. The page needs a new section describing member roles — creating roles, assigning members to roles, and the relationship between roles and content access.
impacts.scope: 1) Add a new section on member roles (creating, viewing, assigning members to roles). 2) Update the introduction to mention that members can be assigned to roles for content access control. 3) Add cross-references to Secure pages and Secure content items pages. 4) Add screenshots of the role management interface in the Members application. 5) Mention that the member listing header sentence should note role-based access as a capability.

impacts.title: Reference - Content item query
impacts.description: The IContentQueryExecutor configuration table documents IncludeSecuredItems but does not mention the new ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames property available on query result containers. The reference page should document this new property in the IContentQueryDataContainer section.
impacts.scope: 1) Add documentation for ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames property in the query result container section. 2) Document that the property is automatically populated by the ContentItemRolesResultProcessor for secured items. 3) Add a note explaining the lazy loading behavior (role data only fetched when at least one result has ContentItemIsSecured=true). 4) Add code example showing how to access ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames from query results.

impacts.title: Content item query API
impacts.description: The page covers building and running content queries. The IncludeSecuredItems option is not discussed on this page (it's only in the reference page), but the new ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames property changes how developers work with secured query results. A section or mention is needed.
impacts.scope: 1) Add a section or subsection about security-related query results. 2) Mention that query results include ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames when IncludeSecuredItems is true and items are role-restricted. 3) Cross-reference the reference page for detailed property documentation.

impacts.title: Reference - Global system events
impacts.description: The events reference page lists ContentItemEvents, WebPageEvents, and HeadlessItemEvents with their event argument properties. The AccessSettings property on event data states now enforces non-null validation. The page should note this constraint where AccessSettings is documented (UpdateMetadata, UpdateSecuritySettings events). Currently mentions 'security settings' at line 500 and 'UpdateSecuritySettings' at line 823.
impacts.scope: 1) In the ContentItemEvents section, update the UpdateMetadata event description to note that AccessSettings accepts non-null ContentAccessSettings values (use ContentAccessSettings.Public() for unsecured). 2) In the WebPageEvents section, update UpdateSecuritySettings event to document the same constraint. 3) Add a note about the three-tier ContentAccessSettings model (Public/Secured/SecuredWithRoles) where event arguments reference security settings. 4) If not already present, document AccessSettings as a property available on event argument state objects.

impacts.title: Member roles
impacts.description: The member roles system (MemberRoleInfo, ContentAccessSettings three-tier model, IContentItemMemberRoleRetriever, IContentItemMemberRoleManager, HasAccess() extension) is a major new feature with no dedicated documentation page. A developer-focused page is needed to explain the concept, API usage, and integration with content security.
impacts.scope: New page covering: 1) Introduction to member roles — conceptual overview of the three-tier access model (Public, Secured, SecuredWithRoles). 2) ContentAccessSettings API — factory methods Public(), Secured(), SecuredWithRoles(roleCodeNames). 3) HasAccess() extension method on IContentItemFieldsSource — code sample for checking access. 4) ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames on query results — code sample. 5) Programmatic role management via IContentItemMemberRoleManager and IContentItemMemberRoleRetriever. 6) Integration with ASP.NET Identity — ApplicationRole, ApplicationRoleStore. 7) Cross-references to business-user pages (Members, Secure pages, Secure content items).

impacts.title: Reference - ContentAccessSettings
impacts.description: ContentAccessSettings is a core type that changed from struct to sealed class. It has static factory methods (Public(), Secured(), SecuredWithRoles()), equality operators, and is used throughout event data, parameters, and metadata classes. A dedicated reference page is needed to document the API surface, breaking change from struct to class, and migration notes.
impacts.scope: New reference page covering: 1) ContentAccessSettings class overview — sealed class, three-tier model. 2) Static factory methods: Public(), Secured(), SecuredWithRoles(IEnumerable<string> roleCodeNames). 3) Equality operators (==, !=) and Equals() behavior. 4) Breaking change note: struct-to-class migration — code relying on value-type semantics (default(ContentAccessSettings), stack allocation) must be updated. 5) Usage examples with event handlers and parameter classes. 6) Null handling: AccessSettings properties now throw ArgumentNullException if set to null — use ContentAccessSettings.Public() instead.

impacts.title: Retrieve content items
impacts.description: Cross-cutting stale content: The 'Content item security' section says items are 'secured to allow access only for authenticated users' — this is now incomplete because items can also be restricted to specific member roles. The existing binary framing (secured=authenticated, unsecured=public) is misleading with the three-tier model. The code sample only checks User.Identity.IsAuthenticated, which is insufficient for role-restricted content.
impacts.scope: 1) Rephrase 'allow access only for authenticated users' to cover the full three-tier model. 2) Update the Razor view code sample (lines 285-296) to show role-aware checking using HasAccess() or ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames. 3) Update the projection example (line 306) to show ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames alongside ContentItemIsSecured.
impacts.note: This stale-fix overlaps with IMP-005 update scope; both target the same page section. The content writer should consolidate these.

impacts.title: Retrieve page content
impacts.description: Cross-cutting stale content: The 'Page security configuration' section says pages are 'secured to allow access only to authenticated users' and the 403 Forbidden description does not distinguish between unauthenticated (401) and insufficient roles (403). Code sample only checks User.Identity.IsAuthenticated.
impacts.scope: 1) Rephrase description to cover three-tier access model. 2) Update Razor view code sample to show HasAccess() as the recommended check. 3) Update the 403 explanation to distinguish NotAuthenticated vs Forbidden scenarios. 4) Mention that AccessDeniedPath now handles both authentication and role-based access denial.
impacts.note: Overlaps with IMP-006; consolidate during writing.

impacts.title: Handle global events
impacts.description: The general events page provides examples and patterns for event handlers. With AccessSettings now enforcing non-null validation across all event data states, any event handler examples or guidance that sets AccessSettings should mention the constraint. The page should cross-reference the updated reference page for the new null validation behavior.
impacts.scope: 1) If any examples modify AccessSettings in event handlers, update them to use ContentAccessSettings.Public() instead of null. 2) Add a cross-reference to the Reference - Global system events page noting the null validation change. 3) Add a note that AccessSettings on event data states is non-nullable.

impacts.title: Reference - CI/CD object types
impacts.description: The CI/CD object types reference should include the new member role binding object types (cms.memberrole, cms.memberrolemember, cms.contentitemmemberrole) if these are CI/CD-supported. AREA-015 mentions ContentItemMemberRoleCustomProcessor for CI processing, and the database schema includes new tables for these objects.
impacts.scope: 1) Check if MemberRoleInfo, MemberRoleMemberInfo, and ContentItemMemberRoleInfo are included in CI/CD processing. 2) If yes, add these object types to the appropriate section (General or Binding object types). 3) Document the custom processor if relevant to CI/CD documentation.

impacts.title: Content sync configuration
impacts.description: Content sync now needs to handle member role bindings and ContentAccessSettings. AREA-014 mentions MemberRoleCompatibilityEvaluator for ensuring role compatibility between source and target instances. The content sync documentation should mention that member role security settings are synchronized.
impacts.scope: 1) In 'Ensure compatibility between instances' or 'Content sync internals', document that member role assignments on content items are synchronized. 2) Add a note about MemberRole compatibility requirements between source and target instances. 3) Mention that role-based access settings are included in content sync payloads.

impacts.title: Secure pages
impacts.description: The page currently describes only binary authentication requirements. The sentence 'Requires authentication – only visitors signed in to the website can view the corresponding page' is now incomplete — pages can also require specific member roles, not just any authentication.
impacts.scope: 1) Update the 'Requires authentication' description to also mention role-based restrictions. 2) Add description of the new role selection UI options. 3) This overlaps with IMP-007; consolidate during writing.
impacts.note: Overlaps with IMP-007; consolidate during writing.

impacts.title: Internal refactoring — ClaimsPrincipalIdentityHelper removal
impacts.description: ClaimsPrincipalIdentityHelper was in the CMS.Membership.Internal namespace and its removal does not affect public API documentation. The helper was replaced with inline LINQ at all call sites with identical behavior. While technically a breaking change for code using Internal namespace types, this is not documented and should not be — Internal namespace types are explicitly unsupported. The Icons.Campaign removal is unrelated to membership.
impacts.scope: No documentation changes needed for the internal refactoring itself. The registration page updates (IMP-001 through IMP-004) already cover the externally visible impact of the role system changes.

impacts.title: Test-only and infrastructure changes
impacts.description: These areas cover: test files (AREA-027 through AREA-032), module auto-registration (AREA-007), admin UI files with no actual diff (AREA-013), recycle bin files with no diff (AREA-016), database schema files with no diff (AREA-017), DancingGoat om.campaign removal (AREA-018/019), API examples with no diff (AREA-020), headless/email with no diff (AREA-021/022), content sync scripts with no diff (AREA-023), frontend ESLint/package bumps (AREA-024), localization string cleanup (AREA-025), and API resolution files (AREA-026). None of these have user-facing documentation impact.
impacts.scope: No documentation changes needed. The code-analyzer confirmed these areas have no actual diffs in this commit or are test/infrastructure only.

contentAccessSettingsStructToClass.description: ContentAccessSettings struct→class affects all code paths. Traced to: registration pages (IMP-001..004), content retrieval pages (IMP-005, IMP-006, IMP-015, IMP-016), business user security pages (IMP-007, IMP-008, IMP-020), events reference (IMP-012), new reference page (IMP-014).

nullValidationHardening.description: AccessSettings null validation affects event handlers and parameter classes. Traced to: events reference (IMP-012), events howto (IMP-017), new reference page (IMP-014).

memberRolesNewFeature.description: New member roles feature requires new pages and updates across member/security/query documentation. Traced to: registration pages (IMP-001..004), content retrieval (IMP-005, IMP-006), business user pages (IMP-007..009), query reference (IMP-010, IMP-011), new member roles page (IMP-013), new reference page (IMP-014).

campaignCleanup.description: Campaign cleanup (om.campaign removal from configs, Icons.Campaign deletion) is a separate change bundled in the same commit. No documentation pages currently document om.campaign in the current version, so no doc impact.

appliedRecommendations.note: API reference first-sentence guidelines applied to all new type documentation.
Method documentation verb-first patterns applied to reference pages.
Parameter description guidelines applied to ContentAccessSettings reference.
Deprecation documentation for IsSecured properties marked obsolete.
Adapted API reference article structure applied to reference pages.
User-perspective how-to framing for member roles page.
Single-goal focus for membership how-to guides.
Admin UI procedure location-first pattern for business user pages.
Adapted Kentico arrow notation for admin UI navigation.
ContentAccessSettings factory method documentation as neutral reference.
Code examples from simple to complex progression.
Backtick formatting for type names, no pluralization of code elements.
Authentication pipeline as concrete developer-configured steps.

blockedRecommendations.reason: Conflicts with INV-style-002 imperative mood titles. How-to titles use imperative form, not 'How to...' prefix.
Conflicts with INV-style-046 which specifies arrow characters (->) not right angle brackets (>).
Conflicts with INV-structure-002/003 which require introductions to answer use case/benefit questions, not 'In this tutorial you will learn' framing.

researchBriefIntegration.notApplicableReason: Tutorial-specific recommendations (REC-006, REC-007, REC-018) and happy-path assumption (REC-017) apply to tutorial/guide content that is not in scope for this impact matrix — no DancingGoat tutorial pages are impacted by this commit.
